questions.question: A company needs to store 500 TB of training data for a deep learning model. The data will be accessed frequently during the initial training phase but rarely afterwards. Which storage solution provides the best cost-performance balance?

explanation.correct: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves data between access tiers based on usage patterns, providing cost optimization without performance impact.

whyOthersWrong.a: S3 Standard maintains high-cost frequent access pricing even after training completes.
whyOthersWrong.c: EFS is significantly more expensive for large-scale storage compared to S3 options.
whyOthersWrong.d: EBS is not suitable for 500 TB of shared training data accessed by multiple instances.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of feature scaling in machine learning?

options.text: To reduce the number of features in the dataset
To ensure all features contribute proportionally to the model
To remove outliers from the dataset
To convert categorical variables to numerical values

explanation.correct: Feature scaling ensures that features with different scales contribute proportionally to the model.

whyOthersWrong.a: Feature reduction is done through dimensionality reduction techniques like PCA.
whyOthersWrong.c: Outlier removal is a separate data cleaning step.
whyOthersWrong.d: Converting categorical to numerical is called encoding, not scaling.

questions.question: Which AWS services can be used as feature stores for machine learning workflows? (Select TWO)

explanation.correct: SageMaker Feature Store is purpose-built for ML feature storage. DynamoDB serves as a low-latency feature store for real-time inference.

whyOthersWrong.a: S3 lacks specialized feature versioning and low-latency access patterns.
whyOthersWrong.d: Redshift is optimized for analytics, not low-latency feature retrieval.

questions.question: A data scientist is training a large deep learning model on Amazon SageMaker and wants to minimize costs. The job can tolerate interruptions. Which approach provides the most savings?

options.text: Use Spot instances with managed spot training

explanation.correct: SageMaker managed spot training can reduce costs by up to 90% using Spot instances with automatic checkpointing.

whyOthersWrong.a: On-Demand instances don't provide cost savings compared to Spot.
whyOthersWrong.c: Reserved instances save 30-60% versus 90% with Spot.
whyOthersWrong.d: Inference-optimized instances are not optimal for training workloads.

questions.question: A company needs to ingest streaming clickstream data and process it in near real-time for ML inference. Which service combination is most appropriate?

options.text: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and AWS Lambda

explanation.correct: Kinesis Data Streams ingests streaming data with low latency. Lambda processes streams in real-time without managing servers.

whyOthersWrong.a: S3 and Batch are for batch processing, not real-time streaming.
whyOthersWrong.c: SQS doesn't provide streaming analytics capabilities like Kinesis.
whyOthersWrong.d: Glue and Redshift are for ETL and warehousing, not real-time processing.

questions.question: A dataset has features with vastly different ranges: age (0-100), income (0-1000000), credit score (300-850). What should be applied before training a neural network?

explanation.correct: Normalization or standardization ensures features contribute equally to neural networks.

whyOthersWrong.a: One-hot encoding is for categorical variables, not numerical scaling.
whyOthersWrong.c: PCA is for dimensionality reduction, not handling different scales.
whyOthersWrong.d: Label encoding converts categories to numbers but doesn't address scaling.

questions.question: When using Amazon SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning, which strategies help reduce costs? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use early stopping to terminate poorly performing jobs
Enable warm start to leverage previous tuning knowledge
Always use grid search instead of Bayesian optimization

explanation.correct: Early stopping terminates unlikely-to-improve jobs. Warm start reuses knowledge from previous tuning, reducing training runs needed.

whyOthersWrong.b: Increasing concurrent jobs speeds tuning but increases costs by running more simultaneously.
whyOthersWrong.d: Grid search is typically more expensive than Bayesian optimization.

questions.question: Which data format is most efficient for storing and querying large datasets in Amazon S3 for ML workloads?

explanation.correct: Apache Parquet is columnar, provides excellent compression, and is optimized for analytics queries.

whyOthersWrong.a: CSV is row-based, doesn't support efficient column pruning, lacks compression.
whyOthersWrong.b: JSON is verbose with poor compression, not optimized for analytical queries.
whyOthersWrong.d: XML is extremely verbose and inefficient for large-scale data storage.

questions.question: A company wants to build a recommendation system for their e-commerce platform. Which SageMaker built-in algorithm is most appropriate?

explanation.correct: Factorization Machines are designed for recommendation systems and work well with sparse user-item interaction data.

whyOthersWrong.a: Linear Learner is for regression and classification, not recommendations.
whyOthersWrong.c: XGBoost is general-purpose but not specialized for recommendations.
whyOthersWrong.d: BlazingText is for text classification and word2vec, not recommendations.

questions.question: Which AWS service provides interactive Jupyter notebooks for data exploration and model development?

explanation.correct: SageMaker Studio provides fully managed Jupyter notebooks for data exploration, model development, and ML workflows.

whyOthersWrong.a: QuickSight is a business intelligence service for visualization, not interactive notebooks.
whyOthersWrong.c: Glue is for ETL operations, not interactive data exploration.
whyOthersWrong.d: Athena is for SQL queries on S3 data, not providing notebooks.

questions.question: Which AWS Glue features facilitate data quality in ETL pipelines? (Select TWO)

explanation.correct: Glue Data Quality allows defining rules to validate data. DataBrew provides visual data profiling to understand data quality issues.

whyOthersWrong.b: Crawlers discover schemas but don't validate data quality.
whyOthersWrong.d: Triggers schedule jobs but don't assess data quality.

questions.question: A model training job requires GPUs. Which SageMaker instance family should be used?

explanation.correct: ml.p3 instances are GPU-optimized for deep learning training workloads with NVIDIA V100 GPUs.

whyOthersWrong.a: ml.t3 are general purpose burstable instances without GPUs.
whyOthersWrong.b: ml.m5 are general purpose instances without GPUs.
whyOthersWrong.d: ml.c5 are compute-optimized but don't have GPUs.

questions.question: A dataset has 1000 categorical features with high cardinality. Which technique would best reduce dimensionality while preserving information?

explanation.correct: Target encoding replaces categories with target mean, reducing dimensionality while preserving predictive information. It handles high cardinality well.

whyOthersWrong.a: One-hot encoding would create thousands of sparse features, making the problem worse.
whyOthersWrong.b: Label encoding creates arbitrary ordinal relationships that may mislead the model.
whyOthersWrong.d: Frequency encoding loses target relationship information.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of Bayesian optimization over grid search for hyperparameter tuning?

options.text: It always finds the global optimum
It requires fewer training jobs to find good hyperparameters
It can only be used with neural networks
It doesn't require defining a search space

explanation.correct: Bayesian optimization intelligently explores the hyperparameter space using previous results, requiring significantly fewer evaluations than grid search.

whyOthersWrong.a: Bayesian optimization finds good solutions but doesn't guarantee global optimum.
whyOthersWrong.c: It works with any ML algorithm, not just neural networks.
whyOthersWrong.d: A search space must still be defined for Bayesian optimization.

questions.question: A company needs to perform complex data transformations on petabytes of data before ML training. Which AWS service is most suitable?

explanation.correct: Amazon EMR with Apache Spark is designed for large-scale data processing and can efficiently handle petabyte-scale transformations with distributed computing.

whyOthersWrong.a: Lambda has a 15-minute timeout and memory limits, unsuitable for petabyte-scale processing.
whyOthersWrong.c: Glue Python Shell is for lightweight scripting, not petabyte-scale transformations.
whyOthersWrong.d: SageMaker Processing is good for ML preprocessing but EMR Spark is more suitable for petabyte-scale general transformations.
